bullets.details: OpenAI is reportedly in final talks to secure approximately $10 billion in new funding from major backers including MGX, Coatue, and Thrive Capital, which would value the AI company at roughly $730 billion.
bullets.topic: OpenAI Nears $10B Funding Deal at $730B Valuation

bullets.details: Disney has formally terminated its $1 billion partnership with OpenAI following the discontinuation of the Sora AI video generator, signaling a retreat from the company's high-profile generative AI ambitions for iconic intellectual property.
bullets.topic: Disney Abandons OpenAI Partnership After Sora Project Collapse

bullets.details: Recent technical breakthroughs demonstrate models increasingly optimizing their own stack, including DeepMind's AlphaEvolve (improving Ramsey number bounds), Moonshot's attention residuals, and efficient OCR models, all yielding better performance with less compute.
bullets.topic: AI Stack Efficiency Advances: Models Optimizing Their Own Algorithms

bullets.details: A GitHub maintainer used a prompt-injection tactic in a repository's `CONTRIBUTING.md` to identify automated agents, discovering that approximately 50% of incoming pull requests were generated by bots, highlighting growing maintenance and integrity risks.
bullets.topic: Prompt Injection Experiment Reveals Over 50% of PRs are AI-Generated Bots

bullets.details: The Trivy project suffered a critical supply chain compromise where attackers injected an infostealer into release versions and Action tags, exfiltrating cloud credentials and environment variables from runner memory.
bullets.topic: Trivy Ecosystem Suffers Critical Supply Chain Attack

bullets.details: NanoClaw has integrated OneCLI's Agent Vault to improve AI agent security. The system proxies requests to prevent agents from directly accessing raw API keys, enabling granular policy enforcement like rate limiting.
bullets.topic: NanoClaw Integrates Agent Vault for Secure Credential Handling

bullets.details: Hardware engineer Andrew 'bunnie' Huang has introduced BIO, an open-source, RISC-V-based I/O co-processor designed as a more compact and performant alternative to the Raspberry Pi PIO, utilizing a standard instruction set for easier development.
bullets.topic: Bunnie Huang Introduces BIO, an Open-Source RISC-V I/O Co-processor

bullets.details: Alibaba and Microsoft have released new compute-efficient, open-weight models (Qwen3.5 small and Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B), signaling a continued industry focus on matching large-model performance with significantly reduced training and compute requirements.
bullets.topic: New Compute-Efficient Model Releases from Alibaba and Microsoft

digest_summary: This week in AI, OpenAI is nearing a massive $10 billion funding round at a $730 billion valuation, even as Disney withdraws from a key partnership. Technical developments are shifting toward efficiency, with models now optimizing their own algorithms and new lightweight models from Alibaba and Microsoft. Security remains a critical focus as the software supply chain faces new threats—highlighted by a Trivy compromise and the discovery that half of GitHub PRs may be AI-generated—prompting new defensive integrations like NanoClaw’s Agent Vault. Additionally, hardware innovation continues with Andrew 'bunnie' Huang’s release of the BIO RISC-V co-processor.
digest_title: OpenAI Valuation Soars, Disney Retreats, and the Expanding Frontier of AI Efficiency and Security
